nuggets.headline: Pete Rango's i-Standard Win Led to Collaborations with Hudson Mowhake and Abjo
nuggets.text: Pete Rango's career as a producer gained significant momentum when he and his main production partner, Parris Pierce, won i-Standard's Music Producers competition in Miami. This pivotal victory opened doors, leading to Pete meeting the producer duo The Renegades and securing sessions that included a placement on Jesse Boykins III’s EP Bartholomew WAVE I, where he collaborated with Hudson Mowhake and Abjo.
nuggets.imageCaption: Pete Rango in the studio, a multi-instrumentalist producer.

nuggets.headline: Pete Rango's XUE Records Aims to 'Disrupt, Uplift, and Make the System Nervous'
nuggets.text: As a culture-builder, Pete Rango's artistry extends beyond the music itself. He founded XUE Records with a clear mission: to disrupt, uplift, and make the system nervous, fostering environments where community, creativity, and technology can truly collide. This philosophy is embedded in his work, driving the innovative spirit behind his captivating pop and indie pop sound.
nuggets.imageCaption: Oms at Play by Pete Rango

nuggets.headline: If 'Oms at Play' hits, explore Jai Paul's innovative electronic pop.
nuggets.text: Pete Rango's innovative blend of pop, electronic, and experimental elements, combined with his distinctive melodic approach, draws a compelling parallel to the elusive brilliance of Jai Paul. Both artists defy easy categorization, crafting unique sonic textures and infectious hooks that feel simultaneously futuristic and deeply soulful, pushing the boundaries of what pop music can be.
nuggets.imageCaption: Jai Paul, an influential artist in electronic and indie pop.

nuggets.headline: Pete Rango's Industry Reach: Unreleased Tracks with Anderson Paak and Kilo Kish
nuggets.text: Beyond his acclaimed solo releases, Pete Rango holds a deep catalog of unreleased material, having penned songs and demos with a roster of A-list talent including Anderson Paak, Kilo Kish, and Foushee. This prolific behind-the-scenes work highlights his influence as both a writer and producer, extending far beyond his own spotlight.
nuggets.imageCaption: Pete Rango in the studio, a multi-instrumentalist producer.

nuggets.headline: From 'In Alcatraz 1962' to 'Savage Planet': Pete Rango's Self-Taught Production Mastery
nuggets.text: Pete Rango's journey into full-stack production was sparked by his high school metal band, In Alcatraz 1962, where the desire to record and mix their own records became a crucial catalyst. This early DIY ambition evolved in college as he embraced electronic music and mashups, shaping the genre-bending, self-produced sound heard on records like 'Savage Planet'.
nuggets.imageCaption: Oms at Play by Pete Rango

nuggets.headline: If 'Oms at Play' has you hooked, dive into KAYTRANADA's soulful, genre-bending house.
nuggets.text: Pete Rango's knack for fusing pop, indie, and dancehall with electronic flair finds a spiritual kin in KAYTRANADA. Both artists are masters of crafting infectious grooves, seamlessly blending house, R&B, and hip-hop into a sophisticated, danceable sound that consistently pushes stylistic boundaries.
nuggets.imageCaption: KAYTRANADA, known for his signature blend of house and R&B.

nuggets.headline: From Metal to Mashups: Pete Rango's Genre-Bending College Transformation
nuggets.text: Before his pop and indie pop fusion, Pete Rango's musical journey took a pivotal turn in college. He discovered electronic music and began intensely mixing different genres, a stark contrast to his high school metal/hardcore band, In Alcatraz 1962. This exploration later led him to Fort Myers, where finding the hip-hop collective PayUp Game shifted his focus towards developing artists and diving deep into hip-hop production.
nuggets.imageCaption: Pete Rango, a versatile artist, producer, and genre-explorer.

nuggets.headline: 'Savage Planet' Reflects Pete Rango's DIY Vision and Collaborative Spirit
nuggets.text: While you're listening to 'Oms at Play' from 'Savage Planet,' understand it emerges from Pete Rango's deep-seated DIY ethos. His journey to master recording, mixing, and production began out of necessity after his early band, fueling his mission to build worlds where creativity and technology collide. The album itself exemplifies this, notably featuring collaborations with Xmane on tracks like 'Planet X' and 'Traag Park,' showcasing his independent, world-building approach to music creation.
nuggets.imageCaption: Oms at Play by Pete Rango

nuggets.headline: If 'Oms at Play' pulls you in, explore Louis Futon's soulful electronic production.
nuggets.text: Pete Rango's ability to fuse indie pop with electronic and hip-hop elements, creating infectious and thoughtful tracks, finds a compelling parallel in Louis Futon. Both artists craft vibrant, genre-bending productions that blend live instrumentation with electronic textures, often featuring soulful vocalists and an undeniable groove that feels both fresh and familiar.
nuggets.imageCaption: Louis Futon, known for his vibrant and soulful electronic productions.
